description:

title: Real Estate Data Pipeline Manager
description:
We are building a county-by-county real estate data pipeline system across New Jersey focused on off-market opportunities.



This is NOT a basic VA/data entry role.



We are looking for a data operator who can think, solve problems, and build structured lead pipelines using multiple data sources.



You will be responsible for identifying, collecting, organizing, and maintaining high value real estate data across multiple counties.



You will work directly with tools like DataSift, SmrtPhone, Smarter Contact, Skip Tracing platforms, PropStream, and REI Reply.



Your job is to turn raw county data into clean, actionable lead lists.



WHAT YOU WILL BUILD (PER COUNTY):



Probate / Heirship Records

Foreclosure / Lis Pendens

Tax Sale / Auction Lists

Tax Delinquency Lists

Code Violations

Condemned / Unsafe Structures

Mechanic’s Liens

IRS / State Tax Liens

HOA / Condo Liens

Utility Shut-Off Lists

Open / Expired Building Permits (City + County)

Fire Damage Reports

Sinkhole / Subsidence Data

Medicaid Recovery Liens

Eviction Records / Detainer Warrants

Child Support Judgment Liens

Quiet Title Suits (Post Tax Deed)



YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES:



Research and locate county-level data sources (websites, portals, public records)

Extract and organize data into structured formats

Clean and deduplicate records before importing into DataSift

Maintain clear documentation for each county (sources, process, notes)

Integrate data into our CRM and marketing systems

Use AI tools (Claude) to assist with data cleaning and structuring

Coordinate with team for consistency across counties



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE:



You MUST have experience with at least 3 of the following:



DataSift

PropStream

REI Reply

Smarter Contact

SmrtPhone

Skip Tracing tools

Real estate lead generation systems



YOU ARE A GOOD FIT IF:



You can figure things out without step-by-step instructions

You are comfortable working with messy or incomplete data

You think in systems, not tasks

You write clean, organized notes and processes

You care about accuracy and consistency



YOU ARE NOT A FIT IF:



You only do basic data entry

You need constant supervision

You avoid problem-solving

You have no real estate data experience



COMPENSATION:



Starting: $4–$6/hour depending on experience

Performance-based increases available

Long-term role with growth into senior data roles



APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS (REQUIRED):



Answer the following:



Describe how you would find and build a tax delinquency list for a county with no obvious download available.

What tools have you used for skip tracing and how do you verify accuracy?

Which of the following have you personally worked with: DataSift, PropStream, REI Reply, Smarter Contact, SmrtPhone?

Provide a brief example of a dataset you organized or cleaned.



Applications without answers will NOT be considered.

title: Sales Coordinator
description:
We are looking for a Sales Coordinator to work remotely from the Philippines, supporting the Managing Director who will be based at the development’s display suite in Australia five days a week.

Your role is to keep the sales pipeline warm, the appointment diary full, and prospective buyers informed and engaged. You are the first point of contact for enquiries and the engine behind the campaign’s day-to-day marketing and lead management. You do not need to close sales — you need to qualify leads, book appointments, and ensure every prospective buyer receives a professional, timely response.

Essential Skills & Experience

Excellent written and spoken English — professional, warm, and polished (you will be communicating directly with high-net-worth buyers)

Minimum 2 years’ experience in real estate sales support, property marketing, or project marketing (off-the-plan experience highly regarded)

Proven experience with appointment setting, lead follow-up, and CRM/database management

 marketing experience — building and sending campaigns via Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign, or similar platforms

Strong proficiency with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Calendar)

Canva proficiency for creating on-brand graphics and marketing materials

Minimum 20 hours a week with a long-term 2-year campaign horizon

Competitive hourly rate paid via Wise (international transfer)

Direct reporting line to the Managing Director — you won’t be lost in a team of 50

A clearly scoped role with defined deliverables and expectations

Opportunity to be part of a high-profile luxury development campaign from launch to completion

A supportive, professional team that values initiative and clear communication

2-week paid trial period with clear deliverables to ensure mutual fit

title: Google Ads Manager
description:
With our recent expansion, we're excited to be hiring lively, hard-working Google Ads professionals to our team! Before we get into the specifics, let us tell you about our company:



Why Join BIG Marketing:



- Work in the US market with a talented team on an exciting mission!

- Paid time off for US Non-Working Holidays.

- Year-End Bonus.

- Company-sponsored learning.

- Comprehensive pay: $5 - $8/hour based on experience and knowledge.

- Opportunity for promotion and pay increases based on yearly performance review.



About BIG:



BIG Marketing & SEO is a digital marketing agency with extensive experience in helping companies grow their profits through SEO and Google Ads. We pride ourselves in delivering the most effective ad campaigns that produce measurable results!



Our company's mission is:



To create a better world through better marketing. 



Some of our core values include:



- Be the best.

- Do the impossible.

- Have fun.

- Make it simple.

- Care for others.



We're not just looking for a Google Ads specialist; we're looking for that perfect person who will be a team player, meet our core values, and win with us.



DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES:



Below is a list of some of the various tasks you'll be required to do to perform well on the job:



- Setting up Google Ads campaigns from scratch (mostly search campaigns and sometimes display campaigns).

- Taking over existing Google Ads campaigns.

- Connecting Google Ads to Google Analytics.

- Monitoring Google Ads and making adjustments to lower CPC.

- Setting up conversion tracking.

- Setting up keywords and negative keywords.

- Making bid adjustments.

- Optimizing landing pages for higher quality scores (you will not be required to design pages).

- Strategizing with the team on offers that could create higher conversions.

- Attending company meetings on Zoom.



REQUIREMENTS



- Proven Google Ads experience: A minimum of 4 years.

- In-depth experience with conversion tracking.

- Proven record of lowering ad costs through optimization.

- Proven record of increasing lead volume through Google Ads, without increasing ad budget.

- Excellent English speaking skills.

- An expert understanding of technical terms related to Google Ads and the web.



- A backup or dual internet connection must be set up.

- At least a quad-core CPU for the computer/laptop you're using (the faster the better!)



BONUS QUALIFICATIONS (Not Required)



If you have any of the following skills, please let us know!



- Landing page development.

- Web design.

-
 Ads management.



IMPORTANT: Our management team feels every person should have a good work-life balance and we try to create a very comfortable remote work environment. That said, it's also important to us that we can also see what work is being done. So we use Time Doctor for very light management and coordination. 



If you've made it this far, please introduce yourself in a 3-minute video and share it when you apply! Please also add why you believe you'd be a good fit for our team by filling out our application form below:



Application Form: 



Website: 



NOTE: We get a lot of applicants on this platform. The applicants we are most serious about considering are those who are willing to introduce themselves in a video and also fill out our application form. We're excited to meet you and look forward to flourishing together!

title: Construction Project Manager
description:
About Us

At Structura Builders, we’re raising the bar on what it means to build with integrity, precision, and care. With offices in San Diego, CA & Minneapolis, MN, we specialize in high-performance commercial and mixed-use projects. As we expand our presence in California & Minnesota and beyond, we're seeking a Remote Construction Project Manager to drive project execution, collaborate across teams, and deliver exceptional results — all while upholding our commitment to quality, safety, and innovation.



The Role

We’re looking for an experienced, proactive, and highly organized Construction Project Manager to join our team remotely and lead project execution from preconstruction through closeout. You’ll be responsible for project planning, subcontractor coordination, and ensuring that projects are completed safely, on time, and within budget. Working closely with field teams and senior leadership, you’ll help ensure Structura Builders delivers on its promise of excellence. 



Key Responsibilities



• Oversee all phases of assigned projects remotely, from preconstruction through final punch and closeout

• Develop and maintain detailed project schedules, budgets, and work plans

• Coordinate and communicate with project superintendents, subcontractors, and field teams to ensure project milestones are achieved

• Review and manage project documentation, including submittals, RFIs, change orders, and meeting minutes

• Ensure compliance with project specifications, drawings, and contract requirements

• Support safety initiatives by collaborating with field superintendents to enforce compliance with OSHA and Structura’s safety protocols

• Collaborate with clients, architects, and engineers to resolve challenges and maintain project 
ntum

• Prepare and review project reports, including financial tracking, budget updates, and work-in-place verification

• Manage project information and communication using Procore, Autodesk Project Management, 
, Microsoft Teams, OneDrive, and Sage Intacct

• Ensure all work complies with ICC Building Codes, including California and Minnesota-specific requirements

• Maintain proactive and transparent communication with all project stakeholders

________________________________________

? What You’ll Bring

• Minimum 7 years of experience working for a U.S.-based company in a project management role for commercial and mixed-use construction

• Proven experience managing complex construction projects from ground-up through closeout

• In-depth knowledge of Procore, Autodesk Project Management, 
, and other project management platforms

• Strong technical understanding of construction processes, project sequencing, and subcontractor management

• Familiarity with ICC Building Codes, with specific knowledge of California and Minnesota standards

• Strong financial acumen, with experience tracking project costs and progress against budgets

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a collaborative, team-focused mindset

• Ability to read and interpret plans, shop drawings, and project specifications

• Commitment to quality, safety, and continuous improvement





Why Structura Builders?

• We build with purpose and precision

• Our leadership empowers remote tea
mbers to make an impact

• You’ll join a tight-knit team that values innovation and collaboration

• We don’t just manage projects — we execute with pride

title: Short-Form Video Editor (CapCut) + Daily Posting (2 Brands – eCommerce)
description:
We are hiring a reliable and fast video editor to create and post short-form content daily for 2 eCommerce brands:

 • Brand 1: Custom personalized products (emotional / gift-focused content)

 • Brand 2: Exotic snacks (fun / viral / product-focused content)



This is a long-term role for someone who is consistent, detail-oriented, and follows instructions exactly.





Your Responsibilities:

 • Edit short-form videos from raw clips (provided)

 • Create 6–10 videos per day total

 • Add light captions/text (based on each brand’s style)

 • Export videos with no watermark

 • Post daily to:

 • TikTok

 • Instagram Reels

 • Pinterest

 • Use provided captions and hashtags

 • Send daily report with posted links





Work Structure:

 • You will work on 2 separate brands daily

 • Each brand has its own time block (no mixing tasks)



Example:

 • 2–2.5 hours - Brand 1 (custom products)

 • 2–2.5 hours - Brand 2 (snacks)





Editing Style (VERY IMPORTANT):



You must follow different styles for each brand:



Brand 1 (Custom Products):

 • Clean, premium, minimal edits

 • Focus on product detail (cards, shine, close-ups)

 • Slow, smooth visuals

 • Light captions only



Brand 2 (Snacks):

 • Faster cuts, more engaging

 • Slightly more energetic style

 • Product reactions, unboxing feel

 • Still clean (no over-editing)



We will provide examples — you must match them exactly.





Requirements:

 • Experience with CapCut (or similar)

 • Familiar with TikTok / Reels formatting

 • Able to follow instructions exactly

 • Consistent daily availability

 • Good internet connection

 • Strong attention to detail





Compensation:

 • Depending on experience

 • 4–5 hours per day

 • Long-term opportunity





To Apply:



Please include the following:

 • A short introduction about yourself

 • Examples of your work (short-form video editing preferred)

 • The editing software you use (e.g., CapCut)

 • Your expected hourly rate

 • Your daily availability (hours and time zone)

 • Your WhatsApp contact number (important to be considered)



We’re looking for someone reliable, detail-oriented, and able to follow instructions consistently.



— Randy

s and Shopify tickets daily (20–50/day)

title: Podcast Production QA & Support Specialist
description:
We’re hiring a Podcast Production QA & Support Specialist for a long-term, stable role on our team.



This is not a beginner role, and it is not just an editing role.



We need someone who understands the full podcast production process well enough to review deliverables for quality, catch issues before they reach the client, and jump into production tasks when needed to help the tea
et deadlines.



You should have hands-on experience with podcast production and content workflows, including editing, show notes, titles, platform-specific packaging, short-form content, and creative assets. You do not need to be the primary person doing every task all day, but you do need to understand the process deeply enough to spot problems quickly and step in confidently when necessary.



We’re looking for someone who is reliable, detail-oriented, proactive, and wants to grow with a team long term.



What You’ll Be Responsible For



• Review podcast deliverables before they are sent to clients or published

• Catch issues in audio, video, show notes, titles, timestamps, graphics, clips, and other content assets

• Make sure work meets internal standards, client expectations, and platform best practices

• Help ensure projects stay on schedule and nothing slips through the cracks

• Support the production team during busy periods or deadline pressure

• Jump into editing, writing, repurposing, packaging, or related production tasks when needed

• Review content with an understanding of the difference between audio platforms, YouTube, and short-form social content

• Check for accuracy, polish, completeness, consistency, and brand alignment

• Help improve workflows, handoffs, checklists, and quality control systems over time

• Communicate clearly with the team and flag issues early



You are a strong fit if you:



• Have real experience working in podcast production or closely related content workflows

• Understand the full production process from raw files to final deliverables

• Can identify quality issues quickly and explain what needs to be fixed

• Have strong judgment, not just task execution

• Can jump into production work when needed and actually know what you’re doing

• Are highly organized and dependable

• Pay close attention to detail without losing sight of deadlines

• Can write clearly in English

• Understand the difference between content for Spotify and Apple Podcasts, YouTube, and short-form social platforms

• Want a long-term role with room to grow



You Should Be Comfortable Reviewing, Giving Feedback and Supporting Work Such As



• Audio and video podcast edits

• Show notes and episode descriptions

• Episode titles

• Timestamps

• YouTube packaging and presentation

• Shorts, reels, and clips

• Social media graphics and supporting assets

• General production handoff quality and completeness



This Role Is NOT For You If



• You do not have hands-on podcast or content production experience

• You only know one part of the process and cannot think across the full workflow

• You need constant direction to spot obvious problems

• You are only looking for short-term freelance work

• You struggle with deadlines, follow-through, or communication

• You cannot switch between reviewing work and executing work when needed

• You do not pay close attention to detail

• You disappear and leave the team hanging



To apply, follow these instructions exactly. Applications that do not follow the instructions will be ignored.



1) Start your application with this exact line:



Quality First, Deadlines Matter



2) Include the following:



• Your full name

• Country and timezone

• Weekly availability

• Tools and software you use

• Internet speed

• Computer specs

• How many podcast episodes or client accounts you can realistically help oversee each week

• Your CV, portfolio, website if you have one



3) Send 3 relevant samples only



These must include:



• At least 1 example of a podcast or interview-style episode you worked on

• At least 1 example of a short-form clip or repurposed asset

• At least 1 example that shows your involvement in quality control, packaging, review, or final delivery standards



For each sample, explain exactly what you did.



4) Answer these questions in full sentences:



• A. When reviewing a podcast episode before delivery, what are the main things you check?

• B. What is the difference between content that is ready for Audio Podcasts versus content that is ready for YouTube?

• C. If a deadline is close and an episode still needs fixes, how do you d
 what to prioritize first?

• D. Tell us about a time you caught an issue before it reached a client or audience. What was the issue, and how did you handle it?

• E. Tell us about a time you had to jump into production work to help a team hit a deadline. What did you do?



5) Include this exact line somewhere in your application:



I am looking for a long-term role and I am comfortable working in both quality control and production support.



Bonus Points If You Also Include



• Before and after examples of work you improved

• Examples of checklists, QA systems, or workflows you helped improve

• Experience working inside an agency or fast-paced production environment

• Examples showing that you understand content strategy, not just technical execution

• Any examples of catching issues in titles, descriptions, graphics, clips, or publishing workflows



This role is for someone who can help us protect quality and keep production moving.



You should be the kind of person who can look at a deliverable, know whether it is truly ready, and step in when needed to help get it across the finish line.



If that sounds like you, apply carefully and follow the instructions exactly.

title: Finance Manager
description:
We are a performance-driven e-commerce and D2C operation focused on building and scaling brands. We are looking for a hands-on Finance Manager to take full ownership of our internal finance function. This is a fully remote, full-time role (150 hours/month) with a flexible schedule: no fixed hours, output-focused.



What you will be doing:

Own day-to-day bookkeeping, journals, accruals, prepayments, and accounting entries

Lead month-end close and deliver accurate management accounts on time

Prepare a monthly Finance Package (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Working Capital, VAT/sales tax summary, variance commentary)

Own all balance sheet reconciliations: bank, payment providers, tax accounts, intercompany, accruals

Manage VAT/sales tax reconciliations and reporting

Act as the main internal point of contact for external accountants, tax advisors, and auditors

Monitor cash position, working capital, and key finance risks

Surface issues early and improve finance processes and internal controls



Requirements:

CPA, ACCA, ACA, or equivalent professional accounting qualification

5+ years of hands-on finance/accounting experience

Strong IFRS knowledge

Solid experience with month-end close, management accounts, and balance sheet reconciliations

Experience with VAT/sales tax reconciliations

Strong Excel or Google Sheets skills

Experience in e-commerce, D2C, or multi-entity businesses is a strong advantage



To apply, please answer the following questions in your application:



1. Do you hold a CPA, ACCA, ACA, or equivalent qualification? Please specify which and when it was obtained.

2. Describe your hands-on experience with month-end close and producing management accounts. What systems have you used and how many entities were you responsible for?

3. What is your experience with VAT or sales tax reconciliations? Please give a specific example.

4. Have you worked in e-commerce or D2C businesses? If yes, describe the business and the finance challenges you dealt with.

5. Are you available full-time (150 hrs/month) and what is your monthly salary expectation in USD?



Please answer each question from your own experience. Short and direct answers are okay. Generic or AI-written answers will not be considered. Thank you for your time.

title: Clinical Intake & Operations Coordinator (Full-Time | Remote)
description:

Schedule: Full-time (8 hours/day, U.S. business hours overlap required)



About the Role



Journey Psyche is a growing multi-state psychiatry practice seeking a highly organized, systems-oriented Clinical Intake & Operations Coordinator.



This is not a casual admin role. This position sits at the front line of patient entry, insurance verification logic, communication discipline, and clinical operations flow.



If you thrive in structure, accountability, and healthcare systems — this role is for you.



Core Responsibilities



• Manage new patient intake (Zocdoc, website, referrals)

• Enter and manage patient threads inside Updox (communication platform)

• Apply structured tagging protocols (no gray threads)

• Verify insurance eligibility and understand:

 • Deductibles

 • Copays

 • PPO / EPO / HMO differences

• Communicate clearly with patients regarding coverage and payment expectations

• Schedule appointments inside Practice Fusion (EHR)

• Monitor refill workflows and appointment follow-ups

• Maintain end-of-day reporting discipline

• Ensure 24-hour response compliance on all patient communications



Required Skills



• Healthcare experience strongly preferred (mental health a plus)

• Strong understanding of U.S. insurance terminology (deductible, copay, coinsurance)

• Excellent written communication skills

• Extremely detail-oriented

• Comfortable working inside structured systems

• High accountability and self-management

• Ability to follow strict communication and tagging protocols



Must Have C1 or C2 English 



Must Be Comfortable With:



• Performance metrics

• Daily tracking reports

• Clear ownership of tasks

• Structured operating procedures

• U.S. healthcare workflows

Bonus If You Have:



• Experience with Updox, Practice Fusion, Alma, or Zocdoc

• Psychiatry or behavioral health exposure

• Prior intake or eligibility verification experience

title: Data Analyst
description:
Data Analyst (SQL + Superset) | Remote | Long Term



We are looking for a highly capable Data Analyst to support a US-based healthcare data team.



This role is focused on exploring datasets, identifying issues, and building reliable dashboards, not just executing predefined tasks.



You should be comfortable working independently and taking ownership of your work.



What you’ll be doing:

- Write advanced SQL queries to analyze and validate data

- Explore datasets to identify inconsistencies, gaps, and potential issues

- Build and maintain dashboards (Apache Superset preferred)

- Perform data QA and proactively flag problems

- Support ad hoc analysis and reporting requests

- Clearly document findings and communicate insights



What we’re looking for:

- Strong SQL skills (joins, aggregations, CTEs, debugging queries)

- Experience with BI tools (Superset strongly preferred, others acceptable)

- Ability to independently explore datasets and find issues

- High attention to detail and data accuracy

- Strong written English communication

- Reliable and responsive (this is very important)



Strong plus:

- Experience working with healthcare data

- Experience with messy or imperfect datasets

- Experience working with US-based clients or teams

- Very comfortable using AI in your tasks



What success looks like in this role:

- You don’t just complete tasks — you identify problems before being asked

- You ask good questions when something doesn’t look right

- You can take a dataset and understand it without heavy guidance



Details:

Remote (anywhere in the world)

15 to 25 hours per week

Rate: $10 to $15 per hour depending on experience

Long term opportunity with increasing responsibility



How to apply:

Please include:

- A short introduction about your experience

- A sample SQL query you’ve written (or describe a project)

- Any dashboard examples (if available)

- Your availability (hours per week + timezone)

title: Phone Research Assistant - Call Landscaping Companies in Phoenix (75-125 calls)
description:
I'm conducting market research on artificial turf installation business in the Phoenix, Arizona metro area and need a VA to make 80-100 phone calls to local turf/landscaping companies.



Your role: Call companies and inquire about services and pricing. Through natural conversation, you'll gather key information about each company (availability, pricing, licensing, service area). This is NOT sales — you're simply having conversations and collecting data.

Note: Some companies on the list are suppliers (sell turf but don't install). For those, you'll just ask about material pricing and move on quickly.



What You'll Be Doing:

Task 1: Customer Inquiry Calls (80-100 calls)



- Call artificial turf companies from a list I'll provide

- First, determine if they do installations or are just a supplier

For INSTALLERS: Ask about availability, pricing, service area, and licensing as a customer would

For SUPPLIERS: Quick call to get material pricing, then move on

- Log all responses in a Google Sheet I'll provide

- Flag high-priority companies based on criteria I'll give you



Task 2: Subcontractor Outreach (15-20 contacts)

- Search Craigslist,
 Groups, and Indeed for independent turf installers in Phoenix

- Reach out to see if they're interested in subcontract work

- Gather info on their experience, equipment, insurance, and rates

- Log everything in a separate sheet



Deliverables:

- Completed Google Sheet with data from all calls

- Priority list of top 10-15 companies worth following up with

- List of 10-15 potential subcontractors



Project Details:

- Total calls/contacts:
Estimated hours: 20-25 hours

- Timeline: Complete within 7-10 days of starting

- Working style: Independent — I'll provide detailed scripts and briefing docs, you execute



What I'll Provide:

- List of companies to call with phone numbers

- Detailed briefing document

- Call scripts and suggested phrases (not word-for-word, just guidance)

- Google Sheet template for logging everything

- Support via text/phone if you have questions



Ideal Candidate:

- Clear, natural English speaking voice (slight accent is fine)

- Experience with phone-based research or cold calling

- Comfortable having casual conversations (not robotic script-reading)

- Reliable, organized, and detail-oriented

- Available to work during US business hours (Arizona is MST, no daylight saving)



To Apply:

Please answer these questions in your proposal:



1. Have you done phone-based research or cold calling before? Are you familiar with Google Sheets? Briefly describe.

2. Are you comfortable posing as a customer to gather information?

3. What hours are you available in Arizona time (PST)?

4. Record a 30-second voice memo introducing yourself (upload to Google Drive or Loom and share the link) — I want to hear how you sound on the phone.



Looking forward to working with you!

Most of my content consists of "talking head" videos where I discuss linguistics, pronunciation, and the mindset behind language learning.



Core Responsibilities:



- Video editing: cutting out bloopers, minimal zooms to emphasize key points, and other typical social media cuts for audience retention.

- Audio: occasional sound effects to emphasize key points, audio cleanup if necessary

- Educational Overlays: Correcting auto-captions, placing diagrams or phonetic transcriptions and other text (I will provide this).



Requirements:



- English proficiency: I teach English pronunciation, so you need to be decent in English, especially since a lot of content revolves around foreign-accented English, your English needs to be good enough to understand other accents

- Communication with me: as it is likely I may need to correct overlaid text or diagrams, so we will need to be in contact a few times a week.

- File management: There will likely be a lot of files we are sharing, so make sure you name the files correctly and place them in the right folders.



How to apply:

Please link your portfolio of video editing work. Also start your message with “phonetics” so I know you’ve read this. If you have any experience with language teaching or the English language, please let me know about that too.

title: E-commerce Virtual Assistant / Product Catalog Specialist (Magento 2 & Shopify)
description:
We are a growing e-commerce business based in Australia, specializing in a wide range of costumes (Halloween, Book Week, Oktoberfest, 1920s styles, etc.). We are running two separate websites—one on Shopify and one on Magento 2.

We are looking for a detail-oriented and experienced E-commerce Virtual Assistant to help us clean up our product catalogs, optimize our listings for SEO, and improve the overall shopping experience for our Australian customers.

Key Responsibilities:

• SEO Product Descriptions: Rewrite existing product descriptions to make them engaging, informative, and SEO-friendly. Strictly NO keyword stuffing. Focus on natural readability and high conversion. Experience with Semrush for keyword research is a huge plus.

• Meta Descriptions: Write compelling Meta Descriptions (under 160 characters) to improve click-through rates (CTR). Note: We do NOT use Meta Keywords, please skip them entirely.

• Image Optimization: Update and modify product "Size Chart" images (using Canva or Photoshop) to ensure they are clear, accurate, and consistent in style. Ensure all images have descriptive ALT text.

• Product Categorization: Reorganize and correctly tag/categorize products across both Magento 2 and Shopify platforms to make navigation easier for customers.

Requirements:

• MUST have hands-on experience with Magento 2. (If you only know Shopify, please do not apply).

• Strong experience managing Shopify stores.

• Excellent written English skills, with the ability to adapt to Australian English spelling (e.g., colour, centre).

• Basic graphic design skills (Canva or Photoshop) for resizing and editing size charts.

• Attention to detail and a strong understanding of on-page SEO best practices (Titles, Meta Descriptions, URL structures).

How to Apply:

To ensure you have read this job description carefully, please include the phrase "Aussie Costume Expert" at the very beginning of your application subject line. Applications without this phrase will be automatically deleted.

Please also include:

1. A brief summary of your experience with Magento 2.

2. An example or link to a product description you have written in the past.

3. Your expected hourly rate.

We look forward to working with you!

title: Short-Form B2B SaaS/AI Video Content Producer
description:
Remote • Philippines-Based • Retainer • £700–£1,500/month



Are you obsessed with AI, short-form video, and creating great content? This role might be for you.



The role in one line: 



You will be a research-driven content producer who turns loose ideas into daily short-form video scripts and presentation slide briefs across LinkedIn, TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.



What You Will Do:



- You will take a loose content idea — sometimes given to you, sometimes your own — and turn it into a complete, ready-to-film package. Each package lives as a single card on a shared Notion board and contains: a script (with hook, spoken word, CTA, and caption copy), and a brief for a separate designer to build the accompanying slides that appear behind the client on camera.



- Some videos will be in faceless format using the client’s voice or AI-generated voice. You need to be comfortable creating quick screen-recordings to showcase the point you’re making. Your videos must be example-packed — never conceptual or high-level.



- The goal is one video posted per day, which means you need to stay ahead of that volume consistently. Typically 1minute long. The client approves every script before filming. Videos may be bulk-filmed in advance, so building a strong queue is a core part of your job.



Content Focus:



 - The content covers GTM strategy, AI features and tooling, and growth — not generic thought leadership. Roughly 50/50 of your output will be evergreen VS timely trend-jacking, so you need to stay on top of what’s happening in the AI and B2B marketing space.



- A critical part of your role is sourcing real data, stats, and concrete examples — especially examples of tools, features, and use cases. This is research-heavy work, and more around cohesive narratives than traditional copywriting. If you love digging into the details and turning them into tight, punchy content, you’ll enjoy this.



What Success Looks Like After 90 Days:



- The client is posting at least one video per day on a consistent schedule across channels.



- Your scripts are tight, well-researched, and require minimal revision.



- You are proactively bringing ideas to the table - not just waiting for direction.



- The Notion board should be a well-oiled queue with scripts and designer briefs always ahead of the filming schedule.



Must-Haves:



- You genuinely use and understand AI tools — not just a passing interest. We want to see that AI is part of how you actually work.



- You understand LinkedIn and TikTok content formats well, with Instagram Reels and YouTube Shorts as secondary platforms.



- You can work from a very loose direction and still produce structured, detailed output without needing your hand held.



- You are a strong researcher — comfortable digging up data, stats, case studies, and real-world examples.



- Your written English is excellent. Natural, confident, and B2B-appropriate.



- You can work autonomously without timezone overlap, as long as your output stays ahead of schedule.



- You are based in the Philippines.



Nice-to-Haves:



These will help you stand out but are not dealbreakers:



- You have experience writing scripts specifically for short-form video — not just blog posts or social captions.



- You are familiar with the B2B SaaS or growth marketing space.



- You are familiar with the AI space.



- You have worked with a personal brand before, not just a company.



- You are comfortable working in Notion & Google apps.



The Setup:



- Retainer position — starting part-time/flexible, with potential to go full-time for the right person.



- Pay: £700–£1,500/month depending on experience and output.



- Fully remote. No timezone overlap required - as long as your queue stays ahead of schedule.



- Zero client interaction. This is entirely behind the scenes.



- Reference creators will be shared with you during onboarding so you know exactly what style and format to match.



- Brand voice guidance will be provided or developed together during onboarding.



If you believe you're the right fit, please send your portfolio and/or resume to 
 with the subject line: "I am your B2B SaaS/AI Video Content Producer."

title: Video and Content Editor
description:
About Funktion Active



Funktion Active is a premium female activewear and athleisure brand where function meets fashion. We create pieces for women who want performance, style, confidence, and versatility in one. Our customer wants apparel that works hard, looks sharp, and feels good in real life — from training sessions to coffee runs to everything in between.



Our brand is built on a few non-negotiables:



Fun — We embrace the grind, support each other, celebrate wins, and bring good energy to the work

Fast & Focused — We move quickly, prioritise what matters, and keep things clean and effective

Funky — We bring creativity, individuality, and personality — always with purpose

Follow-through — We do what we say, communicate clearly, and take ownership

First Class — We care about standards, details, and delivering polished, commercially strong work



Everything we put into the world needs to reflect that — especially our content.



Role Overview



We are looking for a Video and Content Editor who can take large volumes of raw footage and turn it into polished, high-performing, on-brand content for social media, paid ads, and campaigns.



This is not a basic editing role.



We need someone who can shape content that feels sharp, current, clean, and commercially effective — with a strong focus on scroll-stopping, high-performing short-form content.



This role also plays a key part in building and maintaining a strong content reserve, ensuring the brand stays ahead rather than constantly reacting.



Scroll-Stopping & Viral Content Standard



Content should not only look good — it must perform.



We are creating content for fast-moving social environments where attention is earned, not given. Every piece should be designed to stop the scroll within the first few seconds and hold attention through to the end.



This means:



Strong hooks within the first 1–3 seconds

Clean, engaging visual movement

Edits that feel native to the platform

Content that feels current and relevant



We are not chasing trends blindly, but we are also not ignoring them. The expectation is to understand what is working and apply it in a way that still feels on-brand and elevated.



The goal is to consistently produce content that is:



Highly watchable

Easy to consume

Engaging enough to be saved, shared, or replayed

Capable of performing both organically and in paid ads



Good content is not enough. It needs to compete.



Key Responsibilities

Edit raw footage into polished short-form and campaign-ready content

Identify the strongest clips, moments, and creative opportunities from large footage sets

Create clean, engaging edits with strong pacing and flow

Apply colour correction and ensure accurate product colour representation

Format content correctly for Instagram, paid ads, website, and other platforms

Add captions, text overlays, and simple motion elements where needed

Prepare final exports in a clean, organised format for the team

Maintain strong file organisation and naming conventions

Support building and managing a structured, searchable content library

Flag strong unused footage and suggest new content opportunities

Work through large footage banks consistently to build a content reserve

Deliver work that is close to final with minimal revisions required

Identify and prioritise content with strong potential to perform as scroll-stopping social content

Content Focus

Instagram Reels (primary focus)

Gym, fitness, and training content

Paid ad creatives (Meta)

TikTok-style edits

Website and campaign videos

UGC-style and behind-the-scenes content

Product and founder-led content

Expected Output

Up to 150 unique edited pieces per month

Most edits are short-form (~15 seconds)

Work through a 20+ hour footage bank consistently

Maintain a content reserve at least 4 weeks ahead

Creative & Brand Standards



Content should feel:



Fun, engaging, and on-brand

Clean, polished, and premium

Current but not cheap or overdone

Authentic while still elevated



Avoid:



Cheap-looking edits

Overused templates

Cheesy transitions or effects

Sloppy pacing

Visual inconsistency



Reference brands: CSB, Lilo, Hona Active



Workflow & Communication

Work within Google Drive systems for files, briefs, and feedback

Follow provided direction for copy, music, and creative intent

Work independently while staying aligned with priorities and timelines



Communication expectations:



Daily check-in

End-of-day update

Weekly meeting



Daily updates should include:



Completed work

Work in progress

Items awaiting review

Blockers

Next priorities



We value clear, proactive communication — no guessing, no disappearing.



Revisions & Quality Expectations

Most content should require 1–2 revisions max

First drafts should already be close to final

Work should be clean, accurate, and ready to use

What Success Looks Like

Content delivered quickly with minimal hand holding

Consistent editing quality across all outputs

Strong attention to detail and product accuracy

Initiative and problem-solving without constant direction

Content bank stays ahead (minimum 4 weeks)

Files and assets are organised and easy to use

Communication is reliable and proactive

Ideal Candidate

Mid-level to senior editor

Strong experience with short-form and social-first content

Strong eye for pacing, visuals, and content flow

Organised and comfortable managing large volumes of footage

Strong written English and communication

Experience in fitness, activewear, or ecommerce is a plus

Experience with both organic and paid content is a plus

Fast, reliable, detail-oriented, and accountable

Not a Fit If You

Are slow, disorganised, or inconsistent

Need constant direction for simple decisions

Struggle with volume or deadlines

Produce generic, low-quality, or template-heavy work

Miss details or create workflow chaos

Role Structure

Full-time or contract (depending on candidate)

~40 hours per week

Long-term opportunity with growth potential

Confidentiality



You will have access to unreleased campaigns and internal content. Professionalism and discretion are required.



Final Note



We are looking for someone who gives a damn.



Someone fast, sharp, organised, and proud of the work they put out.

Someone who can take raw footage and turn it into content that actually looks like a brand.



Someone who understands:



Fun doesn’t mean messy

Premium doesn’t mean boring

Speed doesn’t mean sloppy



If that sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Admin / Accounts Assistant – Light Weekly Work (Expense Logging & Cleanup)
description:
Hi,



I run a small freelance marketing business in Australia and I’m looking for someone to help with light admin and account support in my system called Moxie.



This is a very small ongoing role (around 1-2 hours per week to start with), helping me keep my account organised and up to date. The work is simple but I need someone reliable and detail-focused.



Tasks may include:



Logging expenses in Moxie (I will
 the expenses to you)

Checking projects and time logs are correct

Cleaning up and organising my Moxie account

Updating client records

Simple invoice / admin checks when needed

General tidy-up of my system



Occasionally I may also need help with:



Building Statements of Work in Moxie

Setting up project templates

Updating project details

Learning more about how to use Moxie features to keep my business organised



The clean-up work does not need to be done quickly.

It can be done slowly over time, whenever it suits you, as long as things stay organised.



I only have a couple of clients at the moment, so this is not full bookkeeping — just basic admin and account support.



If my business grows, the hours may increase over time.



Requirements:



Good attention to detail

Comfortable using online systems

Reliable and organised - happy to communicate via Slack / WhatsApp / Trello

Good written English

Happy to do small tasks carefully



Nice to have (not required):



Experience with Moxie, Xero, or invoicing systems

Experience with admin or accounts support

Experience working with freelancers or small businesses



Hours:



About 1 hour per week to start

Flexible timing

Ongoing work

Potential for more hours in future



To apply, please answer the questions below and include the word Moxie in your application.



1. Have you worked with invoicing, bookkeeping, or admin systems before?

If yes, which ones?

2. This role is very small (around 1 - 2 hours per week).

3. Are you happy with a small ongoing job that may grow over time?

4. Are you comfortable learning a new system (Moxie) and doing slow clean-up / organisation tasks?

5 . How many hours per week are you currently working for other clients?

6. Why do you think you would be a good fit for this type of role?

title: Systems Support Co-ordinator
description:
Systems Support Co-ordinator

at Embrace the Middle East

IT • High Wycombe, England, United Kingdom • Part-time, Hybrid - 1 day per week from the office. 

Analyse my fit

Description

Job title: Systems Support Co-ordinator





Contract basis: Fixed-term, Maternity Cover, part?time (20 hours across 3 days per week).



Reporting to: CRM & Systems Manager



Cluster: Shared Services



Team: IT



Salary: £15,986.30 + £312 working from home allowance (total part-time salary, £16,298.30



per annum). This is based on full-time equivalent of £29,175 per annum.



Job Band: 5



Job description



Purpose:



The Shared Services Cluster provide a service to our supporters, a centralised data management resource and administrative support of various kinds for the whole organisation.



This role combines establishing CRM best practice across the organisation as part of Embrace’s Shared Services team, providing an outward facing service to supporters, with some specific inward facing administrative support to other teams at Embrace.



The role requires close attention to detail, the understanding of data and processes regarding the charity’s central database (CRM), and other systems, consistent with internal procedures and external regulatory requirements (e.g. data protection).





Main responsibilities



1. CRM support



2. Systems Support



3. Supporter Experience



4. Other duties as required





Key tasks:



1. CRM Support:



• Establish best practices across teams as directed by the CRM & Systems Manager, assisting in ensuring Beacon CRM is the first point of knowledge for all staff and providing support and training where appropriate for users across the organisation.



• Advise and work with the CRM & Systems Manager on key CRM tasks within agreed projects to support the team reach its targets, providing insight to help deliver process improvements within the team.



• Undertake data checks of key CRM areas, utilising data tools such as data cleaning dashboards, and highlighting key areas of improvement.



• Provide support to other teams for specific CRM tasks, including but not limited to securing approval for grant payments in line with the Financial Controls Policy, annual bulk instalment creation on the CRM, and the uploading of document templates.



• Ensure supporter data recorded in the CRM is compliant with Embrace’s Data Protection (GDPR) and Vulnerable Person policies and that any safeguarding concerns are raised/handled appropriately.



2. Systems Support



• Understand multiple systems processes across the organisation and help to implement improvements and automations where possible using APIs and no code automation platforms. Help provide resources and expertise to streamline collaborative processes with multiple stakeholders.



• Support the CRM & Systems Manager in ensuring processes across multiple systems are maintained and documented. Provide cover when the CRM & Systems Manager is on leave.



• Assist the CRM & Systems manager in the running of mailing campaigns, providing segmentation to ensure data can be analysed and information can be provided to meet KPIs. • Support insights through reporting analytics using cloud-based data insight tools. Where appropriate modifying data sets using SQL.



• Help support the end-to-end donation process, including the generation of donation pages with HTML.  



• Help and support other teams for specific tasks using Embrace systems including but not limited to updating
 templates and sending bulk
s to supporter/partners, support the running of Partner vetting to ensure compliance with financial sanctions.





3. Supporter Experience



• Provide customer service and data processing expertise to the Supporter Experience Team (SET) working with the CRM & Systems Manager and Shared Services & Legacy Manager to ensure Supporters are provided with exemplary service and that their data is correctly processed and securely stored.



• Undertake a flexible approach, prioritising work according to need, allowing for data processing and supporter service assistance where required during peak times.



4. Other duties as required:



As part of the Shared Service Cluster, be flexible if required to work across multiple teams for projects or short-term goals. Use expertise in CRM processes and methodologies for data capture and insight to deliver cross departmental improvements.



• To play an active part in the wider Embrace team by offering help and support as required.



• Develop excellent working relationships with colleagues throughout the charity.



• Model the Charity’s core values of excellence, accountability, respect, integrity, trust and



compassion at all times.



• Ensure you fully understand and uphold your safeguarding responsibilities and trust inherent with your role.



• To maintain professional boundaries with those you come into contact with and avoid behaviour which might be misinterpreted as inappropriate use of the trust inherent with your role at Embrace.



• Ensure Embrace communications are compliant with GDPR and other relevant regulations.



• Adhere to Embrace’s policies and procedures at all times.



• Undertake any other duties compatible with the role as required.



• To maintain sympathy with the Christian character of the Charity.



• Attend and participate in staff and team meetings.



• Undertake as required any other duties compatible with the role.



• Be proactive in reviewing and evaluating own performance and identifying and acting upon areas for improvement and development.



• Undertake, as required, any other duties compatible with the level and nature of the post.



Person specification



Essential Criteria





General Education



Good general education up to at least A?Level standard.

Skills and Abilities



Excellent computer literacy, including Microsoft Office Online (Word, Excel, Outlook).

Strong experience using a web?based CRM for supporter queries, financial transactions, and basic data analysis.

Experience of managing and updating webforms.

High level of organisational and administrative skills, with excellent attention to detail and accuracy.

Excellent written and spoken English.

Ability to quickly learn new processes and tasks.

Able to use own initiative.

Highly numerate and able to follow instructions for set processes.

Experience Demonstrated experience in customer relations, communication, and customer care.

Experience of no code automation tools.



Experience of clearly and accurately conveying information to a diverse audience with confidence.

Experience using bulk
 tools such as Mailchimp or SendGrid.

Experience processing financial transactions using digital systems.

Personal Qualities



Trustworthy, compassionate, and reliable.

Able to maintain strict confidentiality, especially around sensitive information.

Excellent interpersonal skills.

Self?motivated, able to work under pressure, and able to prioritise effectively.

A strong team player committed to organisational success.

Flexible, adaptable, and with a positive ‘can?do’ attitude.

Sympathy for Embrace’s mission within the Christian faith.

Comfortable working in a faith?inspired environment.

Desirable Criteria



Skills and Abilities



Experience using Beacon CRM.

Experience training non?technical staff in using a CRM.

Project management knowledge.

Ability to use reporting or analytics tools to produce reports from CRM data.

Experience using integration software (e.g., Make or Zapier) to connect systems.

HTML and SQL skills in a business environment.

Experience



Experience working in, or with, a charitable organisation.

Personal Qualities



Creative and innovative approach.

Employment details:



Job Title:



Systems Support Co-ordinator



Contract basis:



Fixed-term, Maternity Cover (20 hours over 3 days per week).



The contract will continue until the end of the maternity leave period.



Reporting to:



CRM & Systems Manager



Location:



Hybrid – remote/home working with the requirement to work at Embrace’s office in High Wycombe once every fortnight for team building purposes.



Hours of work:



Flexi-hours (see details below)



Annual Salary: £15,986.30 + £312 working from home allowance (total part-time salary, £16,298.30 per annum). This is based on full-time equivalent of £29,175.





Benefits at Embrace the Middle East:





• To support every member of staff to work as effectively as possible, Embrace offers remote and flexible working arrangements. This includes flexi-time and home-working arrangements.



• Embrace’s flexi-time framework means that staff can vary, by agreement with their manager, their start and finish times, taking into account the needs of their work and personal preference/circumstance. Staff are required to start work between 7:30am – 10:00am and finish between 3:00pm – 6:00pm. Our core collaborative working hours are between 10:00am to 3:00pm.



• Pension – option to join from day 1. Maximum employer contribution of 10%



• Holiday – 32 days, days made up of 24 personal leave days (including 1 day off for staff’s birthday) and 8 recognised public/bank holidays – rising to 33 days after 5 years’ service and 34 days after 10 years’ service, plus 3 days leave between Christmas and the New Year



• Reimbursement of pre-agreed travel expenses.



• Access to retail discount portal.



• Life Assurance – 3x salary.



• Employee Assistance Programme (confidential counselling, legal and financial advice) – available 24 /7, 365 days a year



• Training and development programme for all employees.



• Commitment to staff health & wellbeing.



Probationary period: 3 months



The closing date for this vacancy is Wednesday 25th March 2026, at 5:00 pm. We strongly encourage you to submit your application before this date.

Please apply via on the following website: 
 



Embrace the Middle East provides equal opportunity for all job applicants and employees and is committed to providing a work environment free of discrimination. We are dedicated to an inclusive culture, and we strive to create a workplace where teams of people with diverse backgrounds, characteristic, perspectives, ideas and experiences work together. We welcome applications from all individuals irrespective of age, race, gender, sexual orientation, ethnicity, religion or belief, disability. If you require any support during the application or interview process, please let us know in your online response via the link above. To monitor diversity in our recruitment practices you will be invited to complete an anonymous Equality, Diversity and Inclusion questionnaire as part of the application process.



Please note that in order to be considered for this position you need to have the right to work in the UK, either through nationality or relevant work visa.



The successful candidate will be required to complete a medical questionnaire and undertake a disclosure check as part of the Charity’s safer recruitment policy.

title: AI social media manager
description:
Social Media Manager – AI Content Creator (Full-Time)



We are looking for a highly skilled and detail-oriented Social Media Manager with strong expertise in AI-driven content creation. In this full-time role, you will be responsible for producing high-quality short-form video content optimized for platforms such as Instagram and TikTok.



You must have advanced experience working with modern AI tools (higgsfield.ai) and be capable of creating realistic, engaging, and visually appealing content based on human-like AI concepts. The ideal candidate has a strong understanding of viral social media formats, excellent editing skills, and the ability to consistently deliver polished, high-performing content.



Responsibilities

 • Create high-quality short-form videos using AI tools

 • Work with human-like AI concepts and ensure realistic, visually appealing results

 • Edit videos professionally (cuts, pacing, subtitles, text overlays, transitions)

 • Optimize content for engagement and performance on Instagram and TikTok

 • Follow content guidelines and maintain consistent visual quality

 • Stay up to date with social media trends and adapt content accordingly

 • Deliver content on a consistent schedule

 • Collaborate to improve creative direction and performance



Requirements

 • Strong experience with AI content creation tools (image and video generation)

 • Advanced video editing skills (cuts, subtitles, timing, formatting for vertical video)

 • Experience creating viral or high-engagement social media content

 • Strong understanding of Instagram and TikTok formats and trends

 • High attention to detail and quality standards

 • Ability to follow creative briefs precisely

 • Reliable, organized, and able to work independently

 • Fluent in English



Nice to Have

 • Experience creating content for personal brands or online businesses

 • Understanding of performance-driven content strategies

 • Experience with CapCut, Premiere, After Effects, or similar tools

 • Experience working with realistic AI characters or avatars



Position Details

 • Full-time remote position

 • Long-term collaboration opportunity

 • Performance-based growth potential



If you are experienced in AI content creation and can consistently deliver high-quality, engaging videos, we would like to work with you.



Contact me with your Application on

title: Remote Construction Estimator (Australian Residential Projects)
description:
Remote Construction Estimator (Australian Residential Projects)



Full-Time | Work From Home | Philippines





evoutsourcing is looking for a Construction Estimator / Quantity Takeoff Specialist to support an Australian modular home builder.





The role focuses on performing accurate quantity takeoffs from architectural drawings and maintaining master estimates for residential home designs.





This is a long-term full-time remote position working with an Australian pre-construction team.







Key Responsibilities



• Perform quantity takeoffs from architectural drawings



• Measure and calculate construction quantities including:



    Slab areas

    Roof areas

    External wall areas

    Cladding quantities

    Ceiling areas

    Tile areas (wet areas)

    Flooring quantities (hybrid flooring, carpet)

    Skirting and architraves

    Plumbing fixtures

    Joinery quantities where applicable.



• Maintain organized takeoff sheets



• Review revised drawings and adjust quantities for client design changes



• Input quantities into construction estimating software



• Maintain master quantity schedules for house designs







Requirements



• Degree in Civil Engineering, Architecture, or Construction Management



• 2+ years experience in estimating or quantity takeoffs



• Strong ability to read architectural plans



• High attention to detail and accuracy



• Strong Excel skills



• Reliable internet connection for remote work





Preferred (Not Required)



• Experience with takeoff software (Bluebeam, PlanSwift, CostX, etc.)



• Experience working on Australian, US, or international residential projects





Work Setup



• Full-time (40 hours/week)



• Permanent work-from-home



• Australian team collaboration





How to Apply



Please submit:



    Resume

    Sample quantity takeoff sheet (if available)

    Brief description of your estimating experience



    Shortlisted candidates will complete a short quantity takeoff test from a residential house plan.

title: Virtual Assistant
description:
Virtual Assistant – Crestline Digital (Remote | Full-Time)

About Crestline Digital

Crestline Digital is a U.S.-based web agency founded in 2019 and headquartered in Austin, Texas. We specialize in helping small and medium-sized businesses in Europe build a professional online presence — websites, local SEO, and digital outreach. Over the past few years we've grown to a team of 20+ people working fully remotely across the globe, serving hundreds of clients in Italy, Spain, and France. Our model is simple: we find businesses that need a website, we build it, we deliver it. Fast, clean, no fluff.

We're not a startup figuring things out — we have systems, we have clients, and we're expanding our team to keep up with demand.

The Role

We're hiring a full-time Virtual Assistant to support our Italy operations team. You'll work directly with the operations manager on day-to-day tasks. This is a stable, long-term role for someone who's organized, communicates well, and wants to grow with a company that's genuinely scaling.

What You'll Be Doing



Building simple business websites using our internal tools (full training provided)

Researching and organizing business leads via Google and spreadsheets

Keeping Google Sheets trackers updated and accurate

Supporting outreach campaigns and daily operations

Communicating with the team via
 and messaging tools



What We're Looking For



Strong written English

Comfortable with Google Sheets / Excel

Fast learner — we use tools like Lovable, 
, and others

Reliable, consistent, proactive communicator

Prior VA or web experience is a plus but not required — we train the right person



What We Offer



?25,000/month fixed salary

Stable, long-term position (we don't do short contracts)

100% remote — work from home

Clear daily targets and structured workflow

Supportive team with real room to grow



To Apply

Send a short message (3–5 sentences) telling us who you are and why this role is a good fit for you. Write AUSTIN in your subject line so we know you read this post.

title: Marketing Automation & Funnel Specialist (GoHighLevel + ClickFunnels)
description:
**Overview**



I’m looking for a skilled Marketing Automation & Funnel Specialist to help set up and optimize the technical systems behind my online education and coaching business.



The main goal of this role is to build and connect the systems that power our marketing funnel — including CRM setup, automation workflows, webinar funnels, and integrations between platforms.



This will start as a short-term setup project, with the potential for ongoing support as the business scales.



**Primary Responsibilities**



CRM Setup



* Set up and organize our CRM inside GoHighLevel

* Create pipeline stages to track leads from registration to purchase

* Set up tagging and segmentation for leads



Marketing Automation



* Build automation workflows for
 and SMS follow-ups

* Set up webinar reminder sequences

* Create post-webinar follow-up campaigns

* Ensure leads move properly through the pipeline



Funnel Optimization



* Audit and improve our existing ClickFunnels lead generation funnel

* Convert the current waitlist funnel into a webinar registration funnel

* Build confirmation, replay, and offer pages

* Connect funnels to CRM and automation workflows



Platform Integrations



* Connect ClickFunnels with GoHighLevel

* Ensure proper tracking and lead capture

* Integrate
 and SMS systems

* Troubleshoot and fix any technical issues



Testing & Optimization



* Test funnel flow from registration to purchase

* Ensure all automations fire correctly

* Fix errors or broken integrations

* Prepare the funnel for paid traffic campaigns



**Tools You Will Work With**



* GoHighLevel (CRM & marketing automation)

* ClickFunnels (sales funnels)

*
 marketing systems

* SMS automation

* Zapier or other integration tools if needed



**Project Scope**

Initial system setup and automation build for a webinar-based marketing funnel launching later this year.



**Hours**

Estimated 5–15 hours for initial setup, with potential ongoing support.



**Compensation**

Open to hourly or fixed-price depending on experience.



**Ideal Candidate**



* Strong experience with GoHighLevel

* Experience building marketing funnels

* Experience with webinar funnels

* Familiar with CRM automation workflows

* Strong troubleshooting skills

* Organized and detail-oriented



**Bonus Experience**



* Working with coaching or online education businesses

* Experience setting up webinar funnels

* Experience integrating multiple marketing tools



**To Apply**

Please include:



1. Your experience with GoHighLevel

2. Examples of funnels or automations you’ve built

3. Tools you’ve integrated in past projects

4. Your availability over the next few weeks

title: Paralegal/Attorney – Criminal Defense
description:
Position Overview:

We are seeking a skilled and detail-oriented Paralegal or Attorney to support our criminal defense practice. This role requires in-depth knowledge of criminal law, including motion preparation, legal research, and document management. The ideal candidate will be responsible for preparing motions, drafting legal documents, and ensuring compliance with legal procedures. They will work closely with attorneys to ensure the defense strategy is executed effectively, and help prepare cases for court. This is a remote position requiring exceptional written English, organizational skills, and a strong grasp of U.S. criminal law procedures fundamentals.



Key Responsibilities:

• Draft, review, and be prepared to file various legal documents including motions, briefs, and memoranda.

• Prepare appendices for motions, including organizing exhibits, declarations, and supporting documentation.

• Prepare the table of contents for legal filings, ensuring accuracy and compliance with court rules.

• Conduct in-depth legal research on complex criminal law issues and generate a research summary for the attorney.

• Analyze discovery materials to identify critical issues and inconsistencies.

• Prepare memoranda and case summaries to aid in litigation strategy.

• Assist in the preparation of motions for hearings, trials, and appeals, including motions to suppress evidence, dismiss charges, or modify bail.

• Manage case files, ensuring all relevant documents and evidence are properly documented and stored.

• Prepare discovery responses, subpoenas, and other legal documents as directed.

• Coordinate with clients, witnesses, and experts to gather information and evidence for cases.

• Maintain communication with the court, opposing counsel, and clients regarding motions, hearings, and court dates.

• Provide support during trial preparation, including organizing exhibits, trial briefs, and witness lists.

• Ensure all legal documents comply with applicable state and federal rules and deadlines.



Qualifications:

• Paralegal Certificate or Law Degree (JD) required.

• Proven experience in criminal defense law, specifically in motion practice.

• Strong knowledge of court rules, procedures, and legal writing standards.

• Experience preparing appendices, tables of contents, and organizing large case files.

• Excellent research, analytical, and writing skills.

• Ability to manage multiple cases and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

• Strong communication skills and attention to detail.

• Experience with legal research tools (e.g.,Lexis/Nexis and Westlaw) and document management software, such as Clio CRM software.



Preferred:

• Prior experience in criminal defense law or litigation.

• Knowledge of specific motions in criminal defense, including motions to suppress, dismiss, and motions for bond 

        reductions.

• Familiarity with Nevada local court rules and procedures in criminal defense.



Application Process:

Interested candidates should submit the following:

• An updated resume.

• A cover letter detailing your experience with U.S. criminal law and Clio software.

• Two writing samples (legal motions or briefs preferred) with how long it required the applicant to finalize the presented 

        document (in hours) from the time the assignment was given to the document presented.

title: Social Media Manger/ Content Creator
description:
Job Title: Creative Marketing Director / Social Media Manager 

Employment Type: Remote, month-to-month (potential for long-term after 90 days probation)

Company: Harvest Health MD / Her Affirmed Life



Harvest Health MD - www.HarvestHealthMD.com

Her Affirmed Life - www.HerAffirmedLife.com



Position Overview: We’re seeking a creative, data-driven marketing professional to lead our brands’ digital presence and community engagement. This role includes managing social media, planning content, coordinating live streams, supporting community events, and analyzing results to drive impact.



The ideal candidate is highly organized, detail-oriented, and self-directed, with a strong sense of integrity and accountability in all work. They communicate thoughtfully and clearly, meeting deadlines with promptness, while maintaining careful attention to detail in every project. They’ll take a human-guided, evidence-based approach, balancing creativity with verified research and data rather than relying solely on automation and artificial intelligence. Most importantly, they live a life of work-life integration, managing responsibilities efficiently while maintaining wellbeing.



Key Responsibilities:

Master Brand Voice and Brand Stories.

Develop, implement, and manage brand strategy and social media content in alignment with marketing best practices and data and analytics.

Drive local brand recognition and online presence by identifying the most important marketing KPIs and developing content to achieve these goals.

Provide recommendations on brand-appropriate content needed for reels/stories.

Maintain Testimonials and Authority Database.

Maintain online reputation and presence while engaging with social media followers and subscribers.

Research, apply for, and develop new opportunities to promote Brand (i.e speaking, podcasts, and awards). 



Qualifications:

Proven experience in social media management and digital marketing

Skilled in analytics and data-driven decision making

Experience managing live streams and community events

Strong written and verbal communication with creative storytelling

Ability to maintain brand consistency across platforms

Self-motivated, dependable, and adaptable in a remote environment



Benefits:

Fully Remote & Flexible Schedule

Collaborative and supportive team culture

Professional Growth Opportunities – Access mentorship, training, and leadership development

Well-being-centered culture with healthy work-life integration

Performance-based bonuses for exceeding goals

Meaningful, mission-driven work

Long-term growth potential



Salary Range:

$3-7/hr



How to Apply:

Interested candidates are invited to complete the Pre-Interview Assessment and submit the following via the Google Form:

Cover letter and resume

OnlineJobsPH profile link

Portfolio



Google Form Link: https://forms.gle/FCpxX25jT4AX5NN69



Join our team and contribute to two purpose-driven brands—Harvest Health MD and Her Affirmed Life—where excellence, innovation, and compassionate, faith-centered care come together to transform lives. Be part of meaningful work that empowers both patients and women in their journey toward healing and wholeness.



Jamal Lawrence, MD

CEO / Founder

Harvest Health MD

title: Online Course Builder / Course Designer

Curriculum Organization



* Help organize my knowledge into clear modules and lessons

* Assist with structuring a 6-week mentorship program

Course Setup



* Build the course structure inside the course platform

* Upload and organize lesson videos

* Add worksheets and resources

Student Experience

Course setup and curriculum organization for a mentorship program launching later this year.

* Experience building online courses

* Strong organizational skills

* Ability to help structure educational content

* Comfortable collaborating to organize ideas

* Experience helping coaches or creators build courses

**To Apply**



Please include:



1. Course platforms you’ve worked with
